firstAid: Cool the burn under cool (not cold) running water for 10-15 minutes. Apply aloe vera.
imagePrompt: Medical first aid illustration showing cooling a minor body part burn under clean running water, flat vector style, dark slate blue background

firstAid: Cool the burn. Do not break blisters. Loosely cover with a sterile, non-stick bandage.
imagePrompt: Medical first aid illustration showing a sterile non-stick bandage loosely covered over a body part burn, flat vector style, dark slate blue background

firstAid: Call emergency immediately. Do not remove stuck clothing. Cover loosely with sterile cloth.
imagePrompt: Medical first aid illustration showing emergency rescue blankets and sterile dressing for a severe body part burn, flat vector style, dark slate blue background

firstAid: Rest, Ice, Compression, Elevation (R.I.C.E.). Avoid putting weight on it.
imagePrompt: Medical first aid illustration showing rest, ice, compression, and elevation (RICE) for a sprained ankle, flat vector style, dark slate blue background

firstAid: Immobilize the arm with a splint or sling. Apply ice pack wrapped in cloth. Seek medical help.
imagePrompt: Medical first aid illustration showing immobilization of an injured arm using a splint and sling, flat vector style, dark slate blue background

symptoms: Inability to stand or bear weight
firstAid: Immobilize the leg using splints. Keep completely still. Call emergency for transport.
imagePrompt: Medical first aid illustration showing immobilization of an injured leg using leg splints, flat vector style, dark slate blue background

firstAid: Call ambulance immediately. Have the person sit down, rest, and keep calm. Loosen tight clothing. If conscious and not allergic, give aspirin.
imagePrompt: Medical first aid illustration showing a person with chest pain sitting down, loosening collar, and chewing an aspirin tablet, flat vector style, dark slate blue background

firstAid: F.A.S.T. (Face, Arms, Speech, Time). Call emergency immediately. Note the time symptoms started.
imagePrompt: Medical stroke assessment infographic demonstrating Face drooping, Arm weakness, and Speech difficulty checks, flat vector style, dark slate blue background

firstAid: Sit the person upright. Help them use their inhaler (blue reliever). Call emergency if it doesn't improve in 5 minutes.
imagePrompt: Medical first aid illustration showing a person sitting upright and using a blue asthma rescue inhaler puff, flat vector style, dark slate blue background

firstAid: Administer epinephrine auto-injector (EpiPen) if available. Call emergency immediately. Lay them flat.
imagePrompt: Medical first aid illustration showing administration of an epinephrine auto-injector EpiPen in the outer thigh, flat vector style, dark slate blue background

firstAid: Wash hands. Stop bleeding with gentle pressure. Clean with water. Apply antibiotic ointment and a bandage.
imagePrompt: Medical first aid illustration showing washing hands and applying an adhesive bandage to a minor cut on body part, flat vector style, dark slate blue background

firstAid: Apply firm, direct pressure with a clean cloth. Do not remove object if impaled. Seek emergency care.
imagePrompt: Medical first aid illustration showing sterile gauze applying firm direct pressure to a bleeding wound on body part, flat vector style, dark slate blue background

firstAid: Keep the person calm and still. Keep the bite area below heart level. Remove tight clothing/jewelry. Do NOT suck venom or apply tourniquet. Call emergency.
imagePrompt: Medical first aid illustration showing a snake bite puncture wound on body part kept immobilized below heart level, flat vector style, dark slate blue background

firstAid: Wash wound gently with soap and water. Apply pressure to stop bleeding. Apply sterile bandage. See a doctor for rabies/tetanus evaluation.
imagePrompt: Medical first aid illustration showing gentle washing of a dog bite wound on body part with soap and water, flat vector style, dark slate blue background

firstAid: Call emergency. Move to a cool place. Cool the person rapidly with water, ice packs, or wet towels. Do not give fluids to drink.
imagePrompt: Medical first aid illustration showing a person being cooled with wet towels and ice packs, flat vector style, dark slate blue background

firstAid: Move to a cooler place. Loosen clothing. Sip cool water. Seek medical help if symptoms worsen or last > 1 hour.
imagePrompt: Medical first aid illustration showing a person resting in a cool shadow and sipping water, flat vector style, dark slate blue background

firstAid: Move gently to a warm place. Remove wet clothing. Warm the center of the body first with blankets. Give warm beverages if conscious.
imagePrompt: Medical first aid illustration showing a person wrapped in warm blankets sipping warm tea, flat vector style, dark slate blue background

firstAid: Get into a warm room. Soak in warm (not hot) water. Do not rub the affected area. Seek medical attention.
imagePrompt: Medical first aid illustration showing frozen frostbitten fingers being warmed in warm water, flat vector style, dark slate blue background

firstAid: Keep them safe from injury. Cushion their head. Loosen tight clothing around neck. Turn on side. Do NOT put anything in mouth. Time the seizure.
imagePrompt: Medical first aid illustration showing a person on their side with a pillow under their head during a seizure, flat vector style, dark slate blue background

firstAid: Call Poison Control immediately. Do NOT induce vomiting unless instructed. Gather pill bottles/containers for information.
imagePrompt: Medical first aid illustration showing a poison chemical bottle and an emergency phone call to Poison Control, flat vector style, dark slate blue background

firstAid: Perform Heimlich maneuver (abdominal thrusts). If unconscious, start CPR and call emergency.
imagePrompt: Medical first aid illustration demonstrating Heimlich maneuver abdominal thrusts on an adult, flat vector style, dark slate blue background

firstAid: Rest in a quiet, dark room. Apply a cold or warm compress to forehead. Take over-the-counter pain relievers like paracetamol (500mg) or ibuprofen.
imagePrompt: Medical first aid illustration showing a person resting in a dark room with a cool compress on forehead, flat vector style, dark slate blue background

condition: Fever — Possible Viral or Bacterial Infection
firstAid: Rest in a ventilated room. Drink plenty of fluids (water, coconut water, ORS). Take paracetamol every 6 hours. Use a damp cloth to cool forehead.
imagePrompt: Medical first aid illustration showing a person resting with a damp cloth on forehead and a glass of water, flat vector style, dark slate blue background

firstAid: Stay hydrated with warm water, ginger tea, or broth. Steam inhale 2-3 times daily. Gargle with warm salt water. Use saline nasal drops.
imagePrompt: Medical first aid illustration showing a person drinking warm ginger tea and inhaling steam, flat vector style, dark slate blue background

firstAid: Sip warm liquids with honey. Steam inhale. Take cough syrup (expectorant for wet, suppressant for dry). See doctor if cough lasts > 2 weeks.
imagePrompt: Medical first aid illustration showing warm liquids with honey and steam inhalation, flat vector style, dark slate blue background

firstAid: Avoid solid food for a few hours. Take an antacid or omeprazole. Apply warm compress to abdomen. Eat light, bland foods (rice, toast).
imagePrompt: Medical first aid illustration showing a warm compress applied to the abdomen, flat vector style, dark slate blue background

firstAid: Sip small amounts of water, ORS, or coconut water. Take ondansetron if prescribed. Avoid dairy, oily, or spicy foods.
imagePrompt: Medical first aid illustration showing a person sipping ORS solution and resting, flat vector style, dark slate blue background

firstAid: Drink ORS solution after every loose motion to prevent dehydration. Eat bananas, rice, applesauce, toast (BRAT diet). Avoid dairy and caffeine.
imagePrompt: Medical first aid illustration showing ORS drink packets and a glass of water, flat vector style, dark slate blue background

firstAid: Apply calamine lotion or hydrocortisone cream. Take cetirizine (10mg) for itching. Wear loose cotton clothes and avoid scratching.
imagePrompt: Medical first aid illustration showing calamine lotion application on an itchy skin rash, flat vector style, dark slate blue background

firstAid: Do not rub eyes. Flush with clean lukewarm water. Use lubricating artificial tears. Seek ophthalmologist if vision is blurry or painful.
imagePrompt: Medical first aid illustration showing flushing a red eye with clean water streams, flat vector style, dark slate blue background

firstAid: If low sugar (<70 mg/dL): Consume 15g fast sugar (juice, sugar water, candy). If high (>250 mg/dL): Drink water, take insulin if prescribed.
imagePrompt: Medical first aid illustration showing a glass of fruit juice and candies for low blood sugar, flat vector style, dark slate blue background

firstAid: Sit down immediately and rest. Take deep, slow breaths. Avoid salt and caffeine. If BP is extremely high (>180/120) with chest pain, call 112.
imagePrompt: Medical first aid illustration showing a person resting and breathing deeply with a blood pressure monitor, flat vector style, dark slate blue background

firstAid: Apply ice pack for first 48 hours, then switch to heat. Take ibuprofen for pain/inflammation. Lie flat on a firm mattress.
imagePrompt: Medical first aid illustration showing an ice pack applied to the lower back while lying flat, flat vector style, dark slate blue background

firstAid: Rinse mouth with warm salt water. Apply clove oil on cotton to the tooth. Take ibuprofen for pain. See a dentist.
imagePrompt: Medical first aid illustration showing warm salt water rinse and clove oil application, flat vector style, dark slate blue background

imagePrompt: Medical first aid illustration showing cooling a minor left hand burn under clean running water, flat vector style, dark slate blue background

imagePrompt: Medical first aid illustration showing a sterile non-stick bandage loosely covered over a left arm burn, flat vector style, dark slate blue background

imagePrompt: Medical first aid illustration showing emergency rescue blankets and sterile dressing for a severe left leg burn, flat vector style, dark slate blue background

imagePrompt: Medical first aid illustration showing rest, ice, compression, and elevation (RICE) for a sprained ankle, flat vector style, dark slate blue background
